name: Sierra de Montsià - Circular Tour via la Foradada
name_original: Sierra de Montsià - Vuelta Circular por la Foradada
region_intro: Jogging around Ulldecona offers diverse terrain, from the ancient olive groves to the challenging slopes of the Montsià Mountains. The region is characterized by its varied landscapes, including river paths along the Sènia River and routes through the Sierra de Godall. Runners can explore both flat, scenic paths and more rigorous trails with significant elevation changes. This blend provides a range of running experiences suitable for different preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Ulldecona The most popular jogging route is Sierra de Montsià - Circular Tour via la Foradada, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This challenging route ascends through the Montsià Mountains, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Serra de Montsià – La Foradada loop from los Campets, a difficult 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This route also explores the Montsià mountain range, featuring varied terrain and natural rock formations. Local runners also love the Ulldecona Town Center loop from Ulldecona, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through the town's surroundings, often completed in about 42 minutes. This moderate route offers a less strenuous option with minimal elevation gain. Jogging around Ulldecona is defined by ancient olive groves, the Montsià Mountains, and the Sènia River. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy town loops to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ulldecona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1600 runners have used komoot to explore Ulldecona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Ulldecona?
A: Ulldecona offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include everything from gentle town loops to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Ulldecona?
A: Yes, for those seeking a less strenuous option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Town Center loop from Ulldecona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.2-mile (6.7 km) trail with minimal elevation gain, often completed in about 42 minutes. It's a great way to experience the town's surroundings without significant challenge.

Q: What kind of challenging running routes can I find in Ulldecona?
A: For experienced runners, Ulldecona offers several challenging routes, particularly in the Montsià Mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946164257/sierra-de-montsia-circular-tour-via-la-foradada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Montsià - Circular Tour via la Foradada&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.8-mile (14.2 km) trail with significant elevation, offering panoramic views. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreta del Montsià loop from los Campets&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 4.8 miles (7.7 km) with considerable ascent.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Ulldecona area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Ulldecona are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946164257/sierra-de-montsia-circular-tour-via-la-foradada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Montsià - Circular Tour via la Foradada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Town Center loop from Ulldecona&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the La Foia d'Ulldecona, an approximately 18.38 km circular route suitable for jogging.

Q: What scenic points or natural attractions can I see while running in Ulldecona?
A: Running in Ulldecona offers diverse scenery. You can explore routes through ancient olive groves, some dating back millennia, providing a unique 'Walk Through History' experience. The Montsià Mountains offer stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/678413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Arch — View of the Ebro Delta&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2909007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreta del Montsià&lt;/a&gt; summit. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullals de Baltasar&lt;/a&gt;, freshwater springs with unique flora.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails around Ulldecona?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations aren't always marked, many natural trails around Ulldecona, especially those away from urban centers and through the ancient olive groves or along the Sènia River, are generally suitable for running with dogs. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and respect local regulations, especially in protected areas like the Sierra de Godall or near agricultural land.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ulldecona?
A: The mild climate of southern Catalonia makes Ulldecona suitable for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in summer, early mornings or late evenings provide cooler conditions, especially for routes along the Sènia River or in the shade of the ancient olive trees. Winters are generally mild, allowing for comfortable running.

Q: What do other runners appreciate most about the trails in Ulldecona?
A: The running routes in Ulldecona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners frequently praise the region's varied terrain, from the unique ancient olive groves to the challenging mountain paths of the Montsià, and the beautiful views, especially from higher elevations.

Q: Are there running routes that offer views of the Ebro Delta?
A: Yes, several routes in the Montsià Mountains provide spectacular panoramic views that extend to the Ebro Delta. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montsià – La Foradada loop from los Campets&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreta del Montsià – La Foradada loop from Aparcament del Mas de Comú&lt;/a&gt; will lead you to viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/678413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Arch — View of the Ebro Delta&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the coastal plain and the delta's unique landscape.

Q: Can I run along a river in Ulldecona?
A: Yes, the Sènia River, which borders Catalonia and the Valencian Community, features a fluvial path spanning over 50 kilometers. Sections of this path offer excellent opportunities for jogging along the river, allowing you to enjoy the riparian forest and diverse vegetation. These routes often provide a flatter, more serene running experience.

Q: Are there running routes that pass through the ancient olive groves?
A: Ulldecona is renowned for its ancient olive trees, with over 1,500 cataloged specimens. Many local paths and trails weave through these historic groves, particularly around the Arion Millennial Olive Trees Natural Museum. Running here offers a unique cultural and natural experience, surrounded by trees that have stood for centuries.
tips: La Foradada, as the name suggests, is a natural hole in the stone, where you can go inside. It is located in the Serra de Montsià, the southernmost of the Catalan territory
The Mas de Mata-redona is a farmhouse, currently abandoned, located in the Serra del Montsià, in the municipality of Ràpita. It gets the name of the mill called Mata-redona near the farm.
